sections.paragraph:
Γαβριήλ, ὁ indecl. (גַּבְרִיאֵל, God’s valiant one) Gabriel, name of an archangel (Da 8:16; 9:21; En 9:1 al.; TestSol 18:6; ApcEsdr; ApcMos 4; ISyrW 2068; PGM 1, 302; 3, 149 and 535; 4, 1815; 7, 1013 and 1017; 36, 310; Bousset, Rel.3 325–28; Billerb. II 126ff; RAC V 239–45) Lk 1:19, 26; GJs 12:2 (Just., D. 100, 5; for an angel announcing a birth, cp. Judg 13:2ff, which is peculiarly embellished Jos., Ant. 5, 276–84).

indecl. indecl. = indeclinable

En En = I Enoch—List 2

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

ApcEsdr ApcEsdr = Apocalypse of Esdras—List 2

ApcMos ApcMos = Apocalypse of Moses—List 2

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

Bousset, Rel. Bousset, Rel. = WB., D. Religion des Judentums im neutestamentlichen Zeitalter—List 6

Billerb. Billerb. = HLStrack and PBillerbeck, Kommentar z. NT aus Talmud u. Midrasch—List 6

RAC RAC = Reallexikon für Antike u. Christentum—List 6

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

ff ff = (and) following

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 186
